assignmentsTab.thisPolicyHasNoOrganizationSoAssignment: This policy has no organization, so assignment targets cannot be listed.
assignmentsTab.noRestrictionAppliesToAllDeviceRoles: No restriction - applies to all device roles
assignmentsTab.noRestrictionAppliesToAllOperatingSystem: No restriction - applies to all operating systems
assignmentsTab.noAssignmentsYetAssignThisPolicyTo: No assignments yet. Assign this policy to targets above.
assignmentsTab.restrictThisAssignmentToDevicesWithSpeci: Restrict this assignment to devices with specific roles. Leave empty to apply to all roles.
assignmentsTab.lowerValuesOverrideHigherOnesWhenMultipl: Lower values override higher ones when multiple policies target the same device at the same level.
assignmentsTab.scopeOfTheAssignmentMoreSpecificLevels: Scope of the assignment. More specific levels (device) take precedence over broader ones (organization).
assignmentsTab.anOrganizationPolicyAlwaysTargetsItsOwn: An organization policy always targets its own organization. Pick a narrower level (site, device group, device) to scope it further.

breezeDefaultsPage.receivedAnInvalidResponseFromTheServer: Received an invalid response from the server.

complianceStatusTab.resultsTheSummaryBelowReflectsThisPage: results — the summary below reflects this page only.

automationTab.installsTheLatestVersionOfTheSelected: Installs the latest version of the selected software; skips devices that already have it.

backupTab.aWSS3MinIOWasabiBackblazeB2: AWS S3, MinIO, Wasabi, Backblaze B2
backupTab.localDiskNASOrUNCShare: Local disk, NAS, or UNC share
backupTab.allDiscoveredVMsAreBackedUpAutomatically: All discovered VMs are backed up automatically. Exclude specific ones below.
backupTab.backupsAreStoredInTheConfiguredStorage: Backups are stored in the configured storage destination. The agent stages files locally and uploads automatically.
backupTab.allDiscoveredDatabasesAreBackedUpAutomat: All discovered databases are backed up automatically. Exclude specific ones below.
backupTab.backupsAreStoredInTheConfiguredStorage2: Backups are stored in the configured storage destination. The agent stages files locally and uploads automatically.
backupTab.captureRegistryBootFilesAndSystemCompone: Capture registry, boot files, and system components.
backupTab.keyPrefixForOrganizingObjectsInThe: Key prefix for organizing objects in the bucket.
backupTab.forMinIOWasabiBackblazeB2Etc: For MinIO, Wasabi, Backblaze B2, etc.
backupTab.localDiskPathMountedNASOrUNC: Local disk path, mounted NAS, or UNC network share. Path must be accessible by the agent.
backupTab.directoriesAndFilesToIncludeInBackups: Directories and files to include in backups. Agents back up these paths on each assigned device.
backupTab.globPatternsToSkipDuringBackupClick: Glob patterns to skip during backup. Click a common pattern to add it.
backupTab.keepLongerRunningRestorePointsForGrandfa: Keep longer-running restore points for grandfather-father-son retention.
backupTab.theseSettingsStampFutureSnapshotsAtCreat: These settings stamp future snapshots at creation time. Legal hold merges conservatively across applicable backup policies, and the longest immutability window wins. Application protection is enforced by Breeze cleanup jobs unless provider-enforced WORM is explicitly verified.
backupTab.providerImmutabilityIsConfiguredButTheSe: Provider immutability is configured, but the selected storage config does not currently have verified object lock support.
backupTab.applicationLevelProtectionBlocksDeletion: Application-level protection blocks deletion in Breeze cleanup jobs. Provider-enforced WORM requires an S3 config with verified object lock support and still re-validates at snapshot time.
backupTab.leaveBlankToAllowBackupsAtAny: Leave blank to allow backups at any time of day.
backupTab.pleaseSelectOrCreateABackupConfiguration: Please select or create a backup configuration
backupTab.providerImmutabilityCannotBeSavedUntilOb: Provider immutability cannot be saved until object lock support is verified. Retest the config or save with application protection.
backupTab.preventRetentionCleanupFromDeletingFutur: Prevent retention cleanup from deleting future snapshots until an operator explicitly releases the hold.
backupTab.addAtLeastOneBackupPath: Add at least one backup path before saving
backupTab.s3RegionIsRequired: S3 region is required — enter it or use an endpoint that includes it (e.g. s3.us-west-004.backblazeb2.com)
backupTab.secretsUnchangedHint: Leave the masked credentials unchanged to keep the saved keys.
backupTab.sourceSubtitle: What to back up on each device
backupTab.scheduleRetentionSubtitle: When backups run and how long restore points are kept
backupTab.presetWindowsSummary: Backs up C:\Users and skips temp files, browser caches, and the Recycle Bin.
backupTab.presetMacosSummary: Backs up /Users and skips caches, Trash, and local iPhone backups.
backupTab.presetLinuxSummary: Backs up /home and /etc and skips caches and Trash.
backupTab.mixedFleetHint: Presets can be combined for mixed-OS fleets. Paths that don't exist on a device are skipped at backup time.
backupTab.switchTypeBody: Switching clears the type-specific settings you've entered for this policy.
backupTab.destinationInfraNote: Storage destinations hold provider credentials and can be reused by other policies.
backupTab.noDestinationsBody: Create a storage destination to hold snapshots. The bucket or path you set up here can be reused by other policies.
backupTab.encryptionDescription: Store snapshots encrypted at rest in the bucket (SSE-S3 / AES256). KMS keys can be configured via the API.
backupTab.capabilitySelectConfig: Select a storage config to check provider immutability.
backupTab.capabilityS3Only: Provider-enforced WORM is only available for S3-backed configs.
backupTab.capabilityRunTest: Run Test to verify that the selected bucket has object lock enabled.
backupTab.capabilityUnavailable: Object lock is not available for this config.
backupTab.scheduleDaily: Every day at {{time}} in the device's effective timezone.
backupTab.scheduleWeekly: Every {{day}} at {{time}} in the device's effective timezone.
backupTab.scheduleMonthly: Day {{day}} of each month at {{time}} in the device's effective timezone.
backupTab.advancedRetentionTimingSummary: GFS long-term restore points and backup window
backupTab.retentionReadback: Keep backups for {{days}} days with up to {{versions}} versions per device.
backupTab.sourceProfileHint: Profiles are reusable "what to protect" selections managed under Backup → Profiles. One profile can protect files, System State, SQL, and Hyper-V together.
backupTab.createProfilesFirst: Create one under Backup → Profiles, or switch to a custom selection.
backupTab.orgDefaultCardUnset: No default destination set for this organization yet. Mark one below as default, or pick a specific destination.
backupTab.partnerDestinationNote: This policy applies across organizations, so backups go to each organization's default destination. Set a default under each org's backup destinations.
backupTab.destinationRequired: Pick a destination or set an org default
backupTab.setAsOrgDefaultHint: Partner-wide policies and profile links without an explicit destination store backups here.
backupTab.profilesLoadFailedHint: Reload the page to try again. Saving is disabled until the list loads, so an existing profile link isn't overwritten.

complianceTab.notifyUsersAndLogComplianceWarnings: Notify users and log compliance warnings.
complianceTab.noComplianceRuleSetsConfiguredYet: No compliance rule sets configured yet.

eventLogTab.eventLogsAreCollectedFromAllDevices: Event logs are collected from all devices by default. These settings tune what's collected, how often, and how long it's retained — they do not turn collection on or off.
eventLogTab.howLongToKeepEventLogs7: How long to keep event logs (7-365 days).
eventLogTab.eventsBelowThisLevelAreFilteredOut: Events below this level are filtered out.
eventLogTab.howOftenTheAgentSendsLogs1: How often the agent sends logs (1-60 minutes).

helperTab.installAndRunTheBreezeAssistTray: Install and run the Breeze Assist tray application on targeted devices.
helperTab.configureWhichItemsAppearInTheBreeze: Configure which items appear in the Breeze Assist right-click context menu. Exit is always available.
helperTab.enableDeployBreezeAssistToDevicesAbove: Enable "Deploy Breeze Assist to devices" above to apply these options.
helperTab.opensTheWebPortalInTheUser: Opens the web portal in the user's browser.
helperTab.showsDeviceHostnameOSStatusAndLast: Shows device hostname, OS, status, and last check-in.
helperTab.opensTheBreezeAssistChatWindowFor: Opens the Breeze Assist chat window for AI-assisted support.
helperTab.leaveBlankToUseTheDefaultBreeze: Leave blank to use the default Breeze server URL.

maintenanceTab.theSpecificDateAndTimeForThis: The specific date and time for this one-time maintenance window.
maintenanceTab.rebootIfARebootIsPending: Reboot if a reboot is pending
maintenanceTab.duringTheWindowRebootDevicesThatHave: During the window, reboot devices that have a pending reboot. Windows warns the signed-in user ~5 minutes before rebooting; Linux schedules the reboot 15 minutes out with a warning to signed-in users. macOS is not supported.

monitoringTab.howOftenTheAgentChecksWatchedServices: How often the agent checks watched services and processes
monitoringTab.regexToMatchTheEventSource: Regex to match the event source
monitoringTab.regexToMatchTheEventMessage: Regex to match the event message
monitoringTab.alertWhenThisManyEventsOccur: Alert when this many events occur
monitoringTab.monitorRunningServicesAndProcessesAlertO: Monitor running services and processes, alert on stop, auto-restart
monitoringTab.addAServiceOrProcessToStart: Add a service or process to start monitoring.
monitoringTab.alertOnWindowsEventLogMacOSUnified: Alert on Windows Event Log, macOS unified log, or Linux journal events
monitoringTab.addARuleToAlertOnLog: Add a rule to alert on log events matching specific patterns.
monitoringTab.cPURAMDiskThresholdsOfflineDetectionAnd: CPU/RAM/disk thresholds, offline detection, and custom conditions
monitoringTab.addMetricThresholdsOfflineDetectionOrCus: Add metric thresholds, offline detection, or custom conditions.

pamTab.theAgentObservesUACConsentPromptsAnd: The agent observes UAC consent prompts and records them as elevation requests for PAM review. Capture is off by default — enable it here to opt a device's scope into PAM.
pamTab.approvalRulesLiveInThePAMConsole: Approval rules live in the PAM console
pamTab.thisPolicyOnlyControlsWhetherDevicesCapt: This policy only controls whether devices capture elevation prompts. Verdicts (auto-approve, deny, require approval), the request queue, and audit history are managed in the

patchAppRulesSection.blockSpecificApplicationsFromAutomatedPa: Block specific applications from automated patching, or pin them to a maximum version.
patchAppRulesSection.couldnTLoadApplicationsYouCanStill: Couldn't load applications — you can still add by package ID below.
patchAppRulesSection.orEnterAPackageIDManually: Or enter a package ID manually

patchTab.rebootOnlyWhenThePatchRequiresIt: Reboot only when the patch requires it.
patchTab.onlyRebootDuringAScheduledMaintenanceWin: Only reboot during a scheduled maintenance window.
patchTab.selectWhichUpdateRingGovernsPatchApprova: Select which update ring governs patch approvals for this policy. Leave empty for manual-only approvals.
patchTab.patchSourcesDescription: OS updates (Windows, macOS, Linux) are always included. Enable third-party updates to also patch applications like Chrome, Firefox, and Zoom.
patchTab.thirdPartySoftwareUpdatesDescription: Patches third-party applications via winget (Windows) and Homebrew (macOS). Required for the Application Rules below and for ring auto-approval of third-party apps to take effect — without it, third-party patches are never installed by this policy.
patchTab.whenApprovedPatchesAreInstalledOnDevices: When approved patches are installed on devices.
patchTab.manageWindowsUpdateExclusivelyThroughBre: Manage Windows Update exclusively through Breeze
patchTab.suppressesTheEndpointSNativeWindowsUpdat: Suppresses the endpoint's native Windows Update automatic-install channel so updates only flow through Breeze's approval rings — preventing unexpected reboots and patches outside your schedule. Windows-only; Breeze's own scan/approve/install path is unaffected. Reverts cleanly when disabled (a pre-existing admin Group Policy is left untouched).

peripheralControlTab.selectAnExistingPeripheralControlPolicyT: Select an existing peripheral control policy to associate with this configuration policy.

remediationScriptPicker.chooseAScriptToRunWhenThis: Choose a script to run when this rule fails compliance

remoteAccessTab.numberOfSimultaneousTunnelConnectionsAll: Number of simultaneous tunnel connections allowed (1-20).
remoteAccessTab.disconnectAfterThisManyMinutesOfInactivi: Disconnect after this many minutes of inactivity.
remoteAccessTab.forceDisconnectAfterThisDurationRegardle: Force disconnect after this duration regardless of activity.
remoteAccessTab.portsPermittedForProxyTunnelingEnterA: Ports permitted for proxy tunneling. Enter a port number (1-65535).
remoteAccessTab.allowTheBreezeViewerToConnectTo: Allow the Breeze Viewer to connect to this device via the Connect Remote Desktop action (WebRTC).
remoteAccessTab.allowBrowserBasedVNCConnectionsToReach: Allow browser-based VNC connections to reach the login window on older Macs. The agent will enable macOS Screen Sharing on demand when a tunnel opens.
remoteAccessTab.allowRemoteProcessManagerServicesRegistr: Allow remote process manager, services, registry, terminal, and file browser.
remoteAccessTab.streamTheRemoteMachineSClipboardTo: Stream the remote machine's clipboard to the operator's viewer. This is the data-egress direction — whatever the end user copies (passwords, MFA codes, secrets) reaches the operator. Off by default on hosted Breeze.
remoteAccessTab.allowTheOperatorToPasteTheirLocal: Allow the operator to paste their local clipboard into the remote machine. Operator-initiated and lower risk.
remoteAccessTab.allowTunnelingNetworkTrafficThroughEnrol: Allow tunneling network traffic through enrolled agents.
remoteAccessTab.automaticallyEnableProxyCapabilityWhenNe: Automatically enable proxy capability when new devices are discovered.
remoteAccessTab.sessionPromptHelp: What the end user sees when a remote desktop session starts.
remoteAccessTab.promptConsent: Require consent — the user must approve
remoteAccessTab.consentUnavailableHelp: Applies when consent is required but no user is signed in to answer.
remoteAccessTab.showIndicatorHelp: Show the on-screen banner while a technician is connected.
remoteAccessTab.notifyOnEndHelp: Notify the end user when the session ends.
remoteAccessTab.identityLevelHelp: How much of the technician's identity the end user sees. The MSP name is always shown.

securityTab.scanNewAndModifiedFilesContinuously: Scan new and modified files continuously.
securityTab.detectSuspiciousProcessBehaviorAndScript: Detect suspicious process behavior and scripts.
securityTab.useCloudReputationForNewIndicators: Use cloud reputation for new indicators.
securityTab.sendDeviceNotificationsWhenThreatsAreFou: Send device notifications when threats are found.

sensitiveDataTab.selectWhichTypesOfSensitiveDataTo: Select which types of sensitive data to scan for.

sensitiveDataTab.fileExtensionsToScanEmptyMeansAll: File extensions to scan. Empty means all types.
sensitiveDataTab.patternIDsToSuppressFromResults: Pattern IDs to suppress from results.

softwareCatalogPicker.alwaysDeployTheMostRecentVersionAvailabl: Always deploy the most recent version available
softwareCatalogPicker.noSpecificVersionsAvailableSelectLatestA: No specific versions available. Select "Latest" above.

softwareCatalogPicker.chooseSoftwareToDeployWhenThisRule: Choose software to deploy when this rule fails compliance

softwarePolicyTab.selectAnExistingSoftwarePolicyAllowlistB: Select an existing software policy (allowlist/blocklist) to associate with this configuration policy.
softwarePolicyTab.executableRulesInThisPolicyAlsoGate: Executable rules in this policy also gate UAC elevation requests on assigned devices — an allowlist match auto-approves, a blocklist match auto-denies, before any PAM rules run.

vulnerabilityTab.correlateThisScopeSInstalledSoftwareAnd: Correlate this scope's installed software and OS versions against the CVE catalog (MSRC, NVD, Apple) to surface vulnerabilities on the device and fleet dashboards. Scanning is off by default — enable it here to opt a customer's devices in. Correlation runs daily.
vulnerabilityTab.findingsAppearInTheVulnerabilitiesDashbo: Findings appear in the Vulnerabilities dashboard
vulnerabilityTab.thisPolicyOnlyControlsWhetherDevicesAre: This policy only controls whether devices are scanned. Detected CVEs, risk scores, and remediation actions are managed in the

warrantyTab.warrantyAlertingIsOptInDevicesWith: Warranty alerting is opt-in: devices with no warranty policy assigned never alert. Thresholds below apply to fixed-term coverage only — active AppleCare subscriptions renew rather than expire, so their expiry alerts are always suppressed.
warrantyTab.generateAlertsWhenFixedTermDeviceWarrant: Generate alerts when fixed-term device warranties are approaching expiry.
warrantyTab.generateAWarningAlertWhenWarrantyExpires: Generate a warning alert when warranty expires within this many days.
warrantyTab.generateACriticalAlertWhenWarrantyExpire: Generate a critical alert when warranty expires within this many days.

detectionRulesEditor.optionalWhenSetInstallUninstallStatusRef: Optional. When set, install/uninstall status reflects the device's real state instead of the installer exit code, and installs are skipped when the package is already present. All rules must match.

detectionRulesEditor.readsTheFileSVersionResourceWindows: Reads the file's version resource. Windows only — on other platforms status falls back to the installer exit code.
